basics.name: Abdul-Samad Ahmed
basics.label: Student Researcher
basics.summary: Undergraduate student in Kinesiology with a strong focus on clinical research, data analysis, and community engagement. Experienced in advanced physiological research methods, community coaching, and digital health initiatives.

research.name: Herzog Group, Human Performance Lab, University of Calgary
research.summary: Assessed the mechanical properties of papillary muscle fibers in male rats exposed to a high-fat and sucrose diet.
highlights: Measured passive force, active force, and calcium sensitivity using skinned fiber preparation to quantify contractile performance.
Compared muscle function between obese and control groups to identify diet-driven changes linked to cardiac dysfunction.
Evaluated methodological and biological limitations to improve the interpretation of muscle mechanics data.

research.name: Herzog Group, Human Performance Lab, University of Calgary
research.summary: Analyzed mitochondrial protein profiles in female rats to identify diet-driven changes linked to obesity.
highlights: Investigated cardiac mitochondrial function in a high-fat, high-sucrose diet model to isolate early biomarkers of cardiac stress.
Evaluated links between altered mitochondrial characteristics and risks associated with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction in female subjects.
Assessed experimental limitations and biological variability to improve the interpretation and reporting of mitochondrial data.

work.name: Open Digital Health, University of Calgary
work.summary: Led and co-authored foundational studies verifying wearable fitness technologies and neurorehabilitation data capture methods.
highlights: Conducted comparative analysis of step count accuracy between wearable technologies across 1,200 data samples using Python and AI for advanced statistical analysis.
Published findings on discrepancies in fitness tracking accuracy, contributing to digital health knowledge.
Co-authored a study validating a novel neurorehabilitation method using wearable technology for physical ability assessment.
Briefed and trained individuals on data collection protocols, led outreach communications, and represented the research lab on national media.

work.name: Kumon
work.summary: Center management and student support for math and reading curriculums.
highlights: Marked assignments and classwork completed by students.
Maintained a focused learning environment and prepared materials prior to student arrival.
Opened, managed, and closed the center in the owner's absence.

work.name: JummieShotit
work.summary: Supported planning, preparation, and execution of professional photo shoots.
highlights: Assisted with concept development, set design, and setup.
Developed flexibility and patience while working with a wide range of people and needs.

work.name: TJX International
work.summary: Customer service associate focused on issue resolution and retail organization.
highlights: Provided customer service, answered questions, resolved issues, and assisted with product selection.
Maintained an organized and visually appealing store environment.

volunteer.organization: Pediatric Emergency Medicine Research Associates Program, Alberta Children’s Hospital
volunteer.summary: Coordinated the credentialing process for 50+ volunteers and contributed to clinical research spanning pediatric emergency care.
highlights: Oversaw credential audits and ensured compliance with program/ethical standards (e.g., TCPS2 Core Certificates).
Contributed to clinical research in the Pediatric Emergency Department by screening, enrolling, and collecting data.
Contributed over 90 hours of volunteer service across multiple studies focused on pediatric emergency care.

volunteer.summary: Youth and adult athletics coach focusing on skill progression and safety.
highlights: Coached youth groups for badminton and adjusted drills to match skill levels.
Planned sessions that supported fitness goals and improved team coordination.

volunteer.organization: Foundations for the Future Charter Academy
highlights: Coached a multi-grade volleyball team in Grades 9 to 11 and tailored training sessions to improve individual and team skills.
Fostered team unity and leadership by mentoring younger athletes.
NCCP certified for Safe Sport, Making Headway, and ASAA coach information. NCCP #6869236.
Selected for Scholars Academy in recognition of leadership, academic excellence, and commitment to community engagement.
Developed leadership skills by guiding first-year students and attending professional development workshops.

volunteer.organization: Children’s Adapted Physical Activity, Mount Royal University
highlights: Developed and implemented personalized physical activity plans for children with diverse abilities.
Provided one-on-one support to children facing significant challenges.
Built proficiency in data collection, manipulation, and analysis.
Received specialized training in rehabilitation therapy for Parkinson’s patients.

education.institution: Foundations for the Future Charter Academy High School

awards.title: Certificate of Achievement, FFCA High School Dragon’s Den Competition

certificates.name: Making Headway in Sport & Safe Sport

publications.name: Counting What Counts: Ensuring Wearable Step-Count Validity for Effective Public Health Interventions
publications.summary: A study examining the validity of wearable device step-count measurements and their implications for public health intervention effectiveness.

publications.name: Stepping in Place (SIP) as a Novel Physical Ability Assessment in Neurorehabilitation: A Wearable Device-based Validation Study
publications.summary: A validation study of a novel stepping-in-place protocol as a physical ability assessment tool in neurorehabilitation, using wearable devices to capture gait and performance parameters.
